title: Fractional Parabolic Theory as a High-dimensional Limit of Fractional Elliptic Theory
speaker: Mariana Smit Vega Garcia
abstract:
Parallels between elliptic and parabolic theory of partial differential equations have long been explored. In particular, since elliptic theory can be seen as a steady-state version of parabolic theory, if a parabolic estimate holds, then by eliminating the time parameter, one can obtain the underlying elliptic statement. Producing a parabolic statement from an elliptic statement, on the other hand, is not as straightforward. In this talk, we will discuss how a high-dimensional limiting technique can be used to prove theorems about solutions to the fractional heat equation from their elliptic counterparts. 
This is joint work with Blair Davey.

proof_stages.label: Classical Almgren-frequency regularity paradigm
proof_stages.summary: The talk reviews the variational formulation of harmonic functions, proves the classical Almgren frequency monotonicity by differentiating energy and height quantities, and explains how blow-ups with constant limiting frequency yield homogeneous harmonic-polynomial leading terms and hence regularity information.

proof_stages.label: Free-boundary motivation for parabolic monotonicity formulas
proof_stages.summary: Obstacle and related free-boundary problems are used to motivate why frequency and monotonicity formulas are powerful, and the speaker poses the central goal of deriving harder parabolic formulas from elliptic ones.

proof_stages.label: Abstract high-dimensional elliptic-to-parabolic transfer scheme
proof_stages.summary: One constructs maps from high-dimensional elliptic variables to parabolic space-time variables, pulls a parabolic solution back to functions solving nonhomogeneous elliptic equations, proves dimension-dependent elliptic statements, transfers them back, and passes to the high-dimensional limit.

proof_stages.label: Nonfractional heat-equation model case
proof_stages.summary: For the backward heat equation, the random-walk/Gaussian bridge converts high-dimensional Laplacians into the heat operator plus an error, a nonhomogeneous elliptic Almgren inequality bounds the pulled-back frequencies, and local uniform decay of the error allows integration and passage to the parabolic Almgren monotonicity formula.

proof_stages.label: Fractional extension and weighted bridge apparatus
proof_stages.summary: The fractional Laplacian and fractional heat operator are localized via degenerate elliptic and parabolic extensions, after which an adjusted high-dimensional weighted bridge, Gaussian change-of-variables formula, growth assumptions, and parabolic boundary conditions are set up for the fractional problem.

proof_stages.label: Fractional Almgren formula from nonhomogeneous degenerate elliptic inequalities
proof_stages.summary: A family of nonhomogeneous weighted elliptic Almgren inequalities is proved, parabolic boundary conditions are shown to be inherited by the pulled-back elliptic functions, and the exact error formula is shown to vanish locally uniformly so that the known fractional parabolic Almgren monotonicity follows in the limit.

proof_stages.label: Fractional Weiss monotonicity via an enhanced limiting argument
proof_stages.summary: The parabolic fractional Weiss formula requires more than the basic transfer scheme: after applying elliptic Weiss formulas to the pulled-back functions, the proof introduces nearby weighted-harmonic functions and an epiperimetric inequality to control homogeneous-extension terms before returning to the parabolic limit.

proof_stages.label: Fractional parabolic ACF monotonicity
proof_stages.summary: An elliptic Alt-Caffarelli-Friedman formula is extended to a nonhomogeneous weighted setting and then passed through the same high-dimensional limiting procedure to obtain a new parabolic fractional ACF monotonicity formula for two disjoint nonnegative subsolutions.

examples.name: Classical harmonic Dirichlet problem
examples.note: The Laplace Dirichlet problem and its local minimizers are used as a familiar model showing how Almgren frequency, blow-ups, and homogeneity can imply regularity.
examples.engagement_reason: she sets up the harmonic Dirichlet minimization, derives the Almgren frequency monotonicity by differentiating the energy/height terms, and runs the blow-up argument for regularity
